input.background: The ANOVA in Exercises 13.3 found that birth weight of four groups of infants differed significantly according to the smoking status of mothers (P = 0.042). Table 13.3 lists the data for the analysis. Data are also available online in the file SMOK_BW.SAV.
input.question: Incorporate Bonferroni's correction into each of the P-value calculated in part (a).

output.answer:
(b) Bonferroni's method tests

| Null hypothesis          | x̄_i - x̄_j | SE      | P-value (2-sided) |
|--------------------------|------------|---------|-------------------|
| H₀: μ₁ - μ₂ = 0         | 0.0857     | 0.67848 | 1.000             |
| H₀: μ₁ - μ₃ = 0         | 1.1191     | 0.64690 | 0.590             |
| H₀: μ₁ - μ₄ = 0         | 1.6129     | 0.60512 | 0.087             |
| H₀: μ₂ - μ₃ = 0         | 1.0334     | 0.67848 | 0.856             |
| H₀: μ₂ - μ₄ = 0         | 1.5272     | 0.63876 | 0.158             |
| H₀: μ₃ - μ₄ = 0         | 0.4938     | 0.60512 | 1.000             |

Calculated with SPSS (Rel. 11.0.1. 2001. Chicago: SPSS Inc.)

Summary in concise narrative form: The difference between group 1 and group 4 is marginally significant. All other differences are not statistically significant.

meta_info.caption: Smoking and birth weight, post hoc comparisons.
